name: Dick Sturza's Beefsteak Marinara
recipeIngredient: 2 lb Top round beef, fat trimmed
recipeInstructions:
Dick Sturza was all set for a juicy beef Stroganoff; the beef was cubed, the mushrooms were sliced, and all the other necessary ingredients were at hand. Then tragedy struck. The sour cream had gone bad, and there was no time to replace it. He improvised brilliantly by creating this beefsteak marinara, a casserole with satisfying complexity of flavor that can be started well before dinner time and will require little attention before serving.
1. Cut the meat into 3/4-inch cubes.
2. Pour 1 tablespoon oil into a 10-12 inch frying pan over medium-high heat. When the oil is hot, add meat, a portion at a time, cooking until well browned; stir often. Transfer the meat with a slotted spoon to a shallow 3 to 3 1/2 quart casserole.
3. Add 1 tablespoon oil to the frying pan along with onion, garlic, and mushrooms; stir often until the onion is limp, about 10 minutes. With a slotted spoon, transfer the mixture to the casserole. Melt butter in the frying pan, add flour, and stir until the mixture bubbles. Smoothly blend in consomme, wine, tomato paste, parsley, basil, oregano, and tomatoes (break them up with a spoon) and liquid; stir until boiling.
4. Mix the sauce with the ingredients in the casserole. Cover tightly with foil and bake in an oven set to 350°F until the meat is very tender when pierced, about 2 1/2 hours. Serve over hot cooked spaghetti and add salt and pepper to taste. Makes 4-5 cups.
